evals.name: code-exists-generates-specs
evals.prompt:
Set up Techlead for this project. Here are my answers:
1. Goal: Build a task management API
2. Success: Users can create, list, and complete tasks
3. Out of scope: Frontend, mobile apps
4. Priorities: Task CRUD operations
5. Tech stack: TypeScript, Express, Prisma, Jest
evals.expected_output: Detects existing source code. Scans the codebase and generates a non-empty SPEC.md (or specs/ directory) with capabilities derived from the actual source code. Specs include Given/When/Then behavioral descriptions. Does NOT create an empty SPEC.md from the template.

assertions.description: Detects existing source code in the project (mentions finding code, scanning, or similar)
Generates a non-empty SPEC.md or specs/ directory — does NOT use the empty template verbatim
Spec capabilities are derived from actual source code (e.g., task management, CRUD operations) — not invented or generic
Specs include Given/When/Then behavioral descriptions for at least one capability

evals.name: specs-use-outcome-language
evals.prompt:
Initialize Techlead for this project. Here are my answers:
1. Goal: Build a task management API
2. Success: Users can create, list, and complete tasks
3. Out of scope: Frontend, mobile apps
4. Priorities: Task CRUD operations
5. Tech stack: TypeScript, Express, Prisma, Jest
evals.expected_output: Generated specs use outcome language describing what the user sees or gets. Specs do NOT contain API paths (/api/tasks), HTTP methods (GET, POST), status codes (200, 404), function names (TaskModel.findAll), or database schemas (prisma.task).

assertions.description: Spec content does NOT contain API paths like /api/tasks, /tasks/:id, or similar URL patterns
Spec content does NOT reference HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, PUT) as part of behavioral descriptions
Spec content does NOT reference HTTP status codes (200, 201, 400, 404, 500) in behavioral descriptions
Specs describe user-observable outcomes using language like 'user sees', 'system returns', 'user receives', 'list appears' — not implementation mechanics

evals.name: greenfield-uses-empty-template
evals.prompt:
Set up Techlead for this project. Here are my answers:
1. Goal: Build a personal finance tracker
2. Success: Users can log expenses and see monthly summaries
3. Out of scope: Tax filing, investment tracking
4. Priorities: Expense logging
5. Tech stack: Python, FastAPI, SQLite, pytest
evals.expected_output: No source code exists. Creates an empty SPEC.md from the template. Does NOT invent capabilities or generate fake specs.

assertions.description: Creates a SPEC.md that is empty or uses the template placeholder — does NOT contain Generated Given/When/Then specs
Does NOT invent or fabricate capabilities based on the interview answers alone — specs are only generated when source code exists

evals.name: multi-file-threshold
evals.prompt:
Initialize Techlead for this project. Here are my answers:
1. Goal: Build a full-featured e-commerce platform
2. Success: Users can browse products, place orders, and manage accounts
3. Out of scope: Warehouse management, shipping logistics
4. Priorities: Product catalog and checkout
5. Tech stack: TypeScript, Express, Prisma, Jest
evals.expected_output: Detects 6+ capabilities from the ecommerce codebase. Uses multi-file docs/specs/ directory with a README.md index and per-capability files because there are 4+ capabilities.

assertions.description: Detects 4 or more distinct capabilities from the ecommerce codebase (products, cart, orders, users, reviews, notifications)
Creates a docs/specs/ directory (multi-file format) rather than a single docs/SPEC.md — because there are 4+ capabilities
The docs/specs/ directory includes a README.md that serves as an index listing all capability spec files
Creates separate spec files for individual capabilities (e.g., products.md, cart.md, orders.md) inside docs/specs/
